title: NIS2 Business Continuity and ICT Continuity Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(c) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) (Business continuity and crisis management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.24: NIS2 Business Continuity and ICT Continuity Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.24' running on index=itsi_summary, nis2_bcp_test_register.csv, index=itsm (major incidents), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_bcp_test_register.csv
| eval test_date_epoch=strptime(last_tabletop_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since=round((now()-test_date_epoch)/2592000,1)
| where months_since > 12
| join service_name [
    search index=itsi_summary earliest=-90d
    | where severity IN ("Critical","High")
    | stats sum(alert_duration) as downtime_seconds by service_name
]
| eval rto_seconds=rto_minutes*60
| eval rto_breach=if(downtime_seconds > rto_seconds, 1, 0)
| table service_name, months_since, downtime_seconds, rto_seconds, rto_breach
description: Business continuity, crisis management, and ICT continuity are explicit Article 21 themes. This use case monitors declared RTO/RPO adherence during real incidents and exercises, combining ITSI service degradation windows with BCP test completion records.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsi_summary, itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Record BCP/ICT continuity test dates per critical service; (2) map services to ITSI entities; (3) alert when annual tabletop is overdue; (4) measure downtime during sev1 incidents against RTO; (5) integrate with UC-22.2.17 for backup evidence. Indexes required: `index=itsi_summary`, `index=itsm`; Lookups: `nis2_bcp_test_register`, `nis2_bcp_test_register.csv`.
visualization: Table (overdue tests and RTO breaches), Timeline (tests and incidents), Single value (services breaching RTO). Key fields: `downtime_seconds`, `months_since`, `rto_breach`, `rto_seconds`, `service_name`, `test_date_epoch`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsi_summary`, `nis2_bcp_test_register.csv`, `index=itsm` (major incidents).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Record BCP/ICT continuity test dates per critical service; (2) map services to ITSI entities; (3) alert when annual tabletop is overdue; (4) measure downtime during sev1 incidents against RTO; (5) integrate with UC-22.2.17 for backup evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_bcp_test_register.csv
| eval test_date_epoch=strptime(last_tabletop_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since=round((now()-test_date_epoch)/2592000,1)
| where months_since > 12
| join service_name [
    search index=itsi_summary earliest=-90d
    | where severity IN ("Critical","High")
    | stats sum(alert_duration) as downtime_seconds by service_name
]
| eval rto_seconds=rto_minutes*60
| eval rto_breach=if(downtime_seconds > rto_seconds, 1, 0)
| table service_name, months_since, downtime_seconds, rto_seconds, rto_breach
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Business Continuity and ICT Continuity Evidence (Art. 21(2)(c))** — Business continuity, crisis management, and ICT continuity are explicit Article 21 themes. This use case monitors declared RTO/RPO adherence during real incidents and exercises, combining ITSI service degradation windows with BCP test completion records.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsi_summary`, `nis2_bcp_test_register.csv`, `index=itsm` (major incidents). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **test_date_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **months_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where months_since > 12` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **rto_seconds** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **rto_breach** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: downtime_seconds, months_since, rto_breach, rto_seconds, service_name, test_date_epoch.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue tests and RTO breaches), Timeline (tests and incidents), Single value (services breaching RTO).
grandmaExplanation: We keep track of business continuity, crisis management, and ict continuity are explicit article 21 themes. this use case monitors declared rto/rpo adherence during real incidents and exercises, combining itsi serv. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
